sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root רוּם)-

(1) height; what is high, lofty, sublime. Placed after another word in the gen. הַר מְרֹום יִשְׂרָאֵל the lofty mountain of Israel, of Zion, Eze 17:23 20:40 34:14 . בַּמָּרֹום on high, Job 39:18 and מָרֹום in acc. id. Isai. 37:23 , וַתִּשָּׂא מָרֹום עֵינֶיךָ “and thou liftest up thine eyes on high.” Concr. Most High (of God), Psa 92:9 and coll. leaders, princes, Isa 24:4. Poet. very high is also applied to any thing far off. Psa 10:5, מָרֹום מִשְׁפָּטֶיךָ מִנֶּגְדֹּו “thy judgments are very far off from him;” comp. רוּם Isa 30:18.

(2) a lofty, fortified place, Hab 2:9 specially of heaven, Psa 18:17 Isa 24:18, 21 Isa 24:21 40:26 57:15 58:4 Jer 25:30 pl. מְרֹומִים id. Job 16:19 of the lofty seat of Jehovah in Zion, Psa 7:8 of an inaccessible fortress, Isa 26:5. Plur. figuratively, of great honours, Ecc 10:6.

(3) loftiness of mind, pride; adv. Psa 56:3.
